seo_description: Current time in Helsinki, Finland — live EET/EEST clock, DST schedule, and time differences with Stockholm, Moscow, and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Helsinki right now?
faq.answer: Helsinki, Finland uses Eastern European Time (EET, UTC+2) in winter and Eastern European Summer Time (EEST, UTC+3) during Daylight Saving Time in summer. Clocks spring forward on the last Sunday in March and fall back on the last Sunday in October. Helsinki is the easternmost capital city in the EU. The IANA timezone identifier is Europe/Helsinki.

faq.question: What time zone is Helsinki in?
faq.answer: Helsinki is in the Eastern European Time zone (EET, UTC+2 in winter; EEST, UTC+3 in summer). Finland follows EU DST rules. Helsinki shares its timezone with Tallinn (Estonia), Riga (Latvia), Vilnius (Lithuania), Kyiv (Ukraine), and Athens (Greece) in winter. The IANA identifier is Europe/Helsinki.

faq.question: Does Helsinki observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Finland observes Daylight Saving Time as part of EU rules. Clocks spring forward 1 hour to EEST (UTC+3) on the last Sunday in March at 3:00 AM and fall back to EET (UTC+2) on the last Sunday in October at 4:00 AM. The EU has proposed eliminating DST, but until any change is enacted, Finland continues to observe it.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Helsinki and Stockholm?
faq.answer: Helsinki (EET, UTC+2) is 1 hour ahead of Stockholm (CET, UTC+1) in winter. During summer, Helsinki (EEST, UTC+3) is still 1 hour ahead of Stockholm (CEST, UTC+2). The 1-hour gap is constant year-round, as both follow EU DST rules simultaneously.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Helsinki and Moscow?
faq.answer: Helsinki (EET, UTC+2) is 1 hour behind Moscow (MSK, UTC+3) in winter. During Helsinki's summer (EEST, UTC+3), Helsinki and Moscow are on the same time. Moscow does not observe DST (abolished in 2014), so the gap shifts between 0 and 1 hour.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Helsinki and London?
faq.answer: Helsinki (EET, UTC+2) is 2 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1) and Helsinki's summer (EEST, UTC+3), Helsinki is still 2 hours ahead. The 2-hour gap is maintained year-round, as both follow DST on the same EU/UK schedule.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Helsinki?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Helsinki are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EET/EEST, Monday through Friday. Helsinki is Finland's capital and largest city. It is a major tech and startup hub — home to companies like Nokia, Supercell, Rovio, and Wolt. The Nasdaq Helsinki stock exchange operates 9:00 AM–5:30 PM EET. Helsinki is consistently ranked among the world's most livable cities and is a gateway between Western Europe and Russia.
